message:
fix(net): TCP rebind guard, split shutdown cleanup, framed_read EOF decode

TcpSocket::bind now rejects rebind attempts on already-bound sockets
with ErrorKind::InvalidInput, preserving the original local identity.
Previously a second bind would silently overwrite the first, causing
the subsequent listen() to use the wrong address. Test added verifying
rebind rejection and original identity preservation through listen().

TCP and Unix split WriteHalf/OwnedWriteHalf: poll_shutdown now properly
handles the case where the underlying shutdown returns WouldBlock by
re-registering interest, matching the pattern used by poll_write and
poll_flush.

FramedRead: decode is now attempted even after EOF is detected. The
previous code skipped the main decode path when eof was set, going
straight to decode_eof. This missed frames that were already fully
buffered before the EOF arrived — the decoder had enough bytes to
produce a frame but was never asked. The decode_eof path remains as
the fallback for partial frames at stream end.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
